landing.heroDescription: PugPIP analyzes your weaknesses, tells you exactly what to practice each day, and tracks your score improvement — so nothing is wasted.
landing.problem1Desc: You take mock tests. You check answers. But your score stays the same. Because nobody tells you why you got it wrong — or what to fix first.
landing.problem2Desc: Generic answer keys don't explain your writing weaknesses. You don't know if your coherence is the issue, your grammar, or your vocabulary.
landing.problem3Desc: Your test date is approaching. You've spent weeks studying everything equally — instead of focusing on the 2–3 things that would actually move your score.
landing.problemQuote: You don't need more practice. You need the right practice.
landing.solutionDescription: PugPIP is an AI-powered score improvement system that diagnoses your weaknesses, builds your study plan, and tells you exactly what to practice — every single day.
landing.solution1Desc: AI scores your writing and speaking across 4 CELPIP dimensions — Task Response, Coherence, Vocabulary, Grammar. You get specific weaknesses and rewrite examples.
landing.solution2Desc: Set your goal score and test date. The AI builds a 3-phase study plan with daily tasks that shift as your scores change.
landing.solution2Callout: Phase 1: Drills → Phase 2: Mixed → Phase 3: Full mocks
landing.solution3Desc: After 2+ attempts, the AI diagnoses recurring patterns, predicts your next score, and tells you if you're on track.
landing.howItWorksSubtitle: From diagnosis to improvement in minutes, not months.
landing.step1Desc: Complete one writing or listening task. The AI scores it across 4 CELPIP dimensions and identifies your exact gaps.
landing.step2Desc: Based on your weaknesses, the AI builds a day-by-day study plan — targeted drills, practice tasks, and mock tests.
landing.step3Desc: Follow your daily tasks. Watch your predicted score improve. Walk into test day knowing you're ready.
landing.dashboardDesc: Every day, your dashboard shows exactly what to do. No guessing. No scrolling through question banks. Just the tasks that will move your score the most.
landing.dashboardFeature1: Track all 4 sections — Listening, Reading, Writing, Speaking
landing.dashboardFeature2: AI scores writing & speaking across 4 dimensions
landing.dashboardFeature3: Pinpoints weakest parts and recurring patterns
landing.dashboardFeature4: Predicted score and adaptive study plan
landing.comparisonTitle: Not all CELPIP prep is <accent>the same</accent>
landing.compareFeedbackNew: AI scores Task Response, Coherence, Vocabulary, Grammar + rewrite examples
landing.comparePracticeModesNew: Full mock tests + section drills + targeted quizzes
landing.pricingSubtitle: Start for free. Upgrade when you're ready to get serious about your score.
landing.testimonialsSubtitle: Test-takers across Canada reaching their target scores.
landing.finalCtaDesc: Most users see measurable improvement within 2 weeks. Start with a free AI diagnosis — it takes 5 minutes.
landing.finalCtaQuote: "Your CELPIP tutor charges $60/hr and still can't tell you exactly what to fix."
landing.noCreditCard: No credit card required. Stop guessing. Start knowing.
landing.mockupFixDesc: Weak paragraph transitions → Use linking phrases like "Furthermore", "In contrast", "As a result"

pricing.improverDesc: AI-powered feedback to level up fast

dashboard.readingDesc: Emails, schedules, articles, and opinion pieces with comprehension questions.
dashboard.writingDesc: Write an email and respond to a survey question with structured responses.
dashboard.speakingDesc: Respond to prompts covering advice, descriptions, opinions, and persuasion.
dashboard.personalizedRecs: Personalized recommendations based on your test results.

dashboard.takeTestRecs: Take a practice test to get personalized recommendations.
dashboard.tip_listening_exc_0: Focus on the most nuanced inference questions in Parts 5-6.
dashboard.tip_listening_exc_1: Practice with varied accents and faster speech speeds.
dashboard.tip_listening_exc_2: Challenge yourself with academic lectures and panel discussions.
dashboard.tip_reading_exc_0: Practice speed-reading to bank extra time for review.
dashboard.tip_reading_exc_1: Focus on tone/attitude questions that require subtle inference.
dashboard.tip_reading_exc_2: Read editorials and academic papers to sharpen critical analysis.
dashboard.tip_writing_exc_0: Aim for sophisticated cohesion devices (moreover, notwithstanding).
dashboard.tip_writing_exc_1: Experiment with complex sentence structures — conditionals, cleft sentences.
dashboard.tip_writing_exc_2: Proofread for micro-errors: article usage, preposition collocations.
dashboard.tip_speaking_exc_0: Use idiomatic expressions naturally — avoid overuse.
dashboard.tip_speaking_exc_1: Work on intonation and stress patterns for emphasis.
dashboard.tip_speaking_exc_2: Practice impromptu responses to unfamiliar topics under time pressure.
dashboard.tip_listening_str_0: Focus on implicit meaning and speaker attitude in Parts 4-6.
dashboard.tip_listening_str_1: Practice note-taking with abbreviations for speed.
dashboard.tip_listening_str_2: Listen to podcasts at 1.25x speed to build processing capacity.
dashboard.tip_reading_str_0: Work on inference and 'author's purpose' question types.
dashboard.tip_reading_str_1: Practice eliminating distractors in multiple-choice questions.
dashboard.tip_reading_str_2: Read opinion pieces and practice identifying the writer's stance.
dashboard.tip_writing_str_0: Expand vocabulary with less common synonyms and collocations.
dashboard.tip_writing_str_1: Use a wider range of sentence structures (participle clauses, inversions).
dashboard.tip_writing_str_2: Strengthen paragraph transitions with clear topic sentences.
dashboard.tip_speaking_str_0: Develop more detailed descriptions with specific adjectives and adverbs.
dashboard.tip_speaking_str_1: Practice expressing and defending opinions with supporting examples.
dashboard.tip_speaking_str_2: Record yourself and review for filler words (um, uh, like).
dashboard.tip_listening_dev_0: Practice active listening — pause after each part and summarize what you heard.
dashboard.tip_listening_dev_1: Focus on catching numbers, names, and key details on first listen.
dashboard.tip_listening_dev_2: Use the notepad to jot speaker opinions and relationships.
dashboard.tip_reading_dev_0: Read the questions BEFORE the passage to know what to look for.
dashboard.tip_reading_dev_1: Practice skimming for main ideas and scanning for specific details.
dashboard.tip_reading_dev_2: Build vocabulary with CELPIP-level word lists — focus on academic words.
dashboard.tip_writing_dev_0: Master the basic email format: greeting, 3 body paragraphs, closing.
dashboard.tip_writing_dev_1: Practice the 5-18-4 rule: 5 min plan, 18 min write, 4 min review.
dashboard.tip_writing_dev_2: Focus on addressing ALL bullet points in the prompt completely.
dashboard.tip_speaking_dev_0: Use simple response frameworks: state opinion → reason 1 → reason 2 → example.
dashboard.tip_speaking_dev_1: Practice speaking for the full time allotted — don't stop early.
dashboard.tip_speaking_dev_2: Focus on clarity and fluency over complex vocabulary.
dashboard.tip_listening_nds_0: Start with slower audio materials and gradually increase speed.
dashboard.tip_listening_nds_1: Practice identifying the main topic of short conversations.
dashboard.tip_listening_nds_2: Learn to recognize common question patterns (who, what, why, where).
dashboard.tip_reading_nds_0: Build reading stamina — read English articles for 20 minutes daily.
dashboard.tip_reading_nds_1: Start with shorter passages and focus on understanding the main idea.
dashboard.tip_reading_nds_2: Learn common CELPIP vocabulary through flashcards and context reading.
dashboard.tip_writing_nds_0: Practice writing simple, clear sentences before attempting complex ones.
dashboard.tip_writing_nds_1: Learn basic email conventions: formal vs informal tone.
dashboard.tip_writing_nds_2: Focus on spelling and basic grammar — subject-verb agreement, tenses.
dashboard.tip_speaking_nds_0: Practice speaking English daily, even 10 minutes of self-talk helps.
dashboard.tip_speaking_nds_1: Learn and rehearse common opinion phrases and transition words.
dashboard.tip_speaking_nds_2: Focus on pronunciation of commonly mispronounced words in your language.
dashboard.advice_0_content: In the CELPIP test, every section is timed. Practice with strict timers from day one. For reading, spend no more than 10 minutes per part. For writing, allocate 5 minutes to plan, 18 minutes to write, and 4 minutes to review.
dashboard.advice_1_content: CELPIP provides a digital notepad during the listening section. Write down key names, numbers, and relationships as you listen. This is especially crucial for Parts 3-6 where you need to track multiple speakers.
dashboard.advice_2_content: Writing Task 1 always asks for an email. Use a clear structure: greeting, 3 body paragraphs (one per bullet point), and a closing. Match your tone to the audience — formal for a manager, semi-formal for a colleague, informal for a friend.
dashboard.advice_3_content: Memorized responses sound unnatural and score low. Instead, practice response patterns: 'In my opinion... because firstly... secondly... For instance...' These frameworks let you speak naturally about any topic.
dashboard.advice_4_content: Before reading a passage, scan all the questions. This tells your brain what to look for, making you faster and more accurate. Focus on keywords in questions — names, dates, opinions, and cause-effect relationships.
dashboard.advice_5_content: Take at least 3 full-length practice tests under real conditions: no pausing, no phone, quiet room, full timing. This builds stamina for the 3-hour test and reduces anxiety on test day.

test.instructionsSubtitle: Please read the instructions for each section carefully before you begin. The test sections must be completed in order.
test.importantWarning: Important: Once you begin a section, the timer will start. You cannot pause the timer. Make sure you are in a quiet environment and ready to focus.
test.listeningInst1: You will read transcripts of conversations and presentations (simulating audio).
test.listeningInst2: There are 6 parts with 5 questions each (30 questions total).
test.listeningInst3: Select the best answer for each multiple-choice question.
test.listeningInst4: A timer will count down. When time runs out, your answers are submitted automatically.
test.readingInst1: You will read emails, schedules, articles, and opinion pieces.
test.readingInst2: There are 4 parts with varying numbers of questions (27 total).
test.readingInst3: Read each passage carefully before answering the questions.
test.readingInst4: You may go back and change your answers within each part.
test.writingInst1: Task 1: Write an email based on a given scenario (150–200 words).
test.writingInst2: Task 2: Respond to a survey question (150–200 words).
test.writingInst3: A word counter will help you track your response length.
test.writingInst4: Both tasks share the total time allocation.
test.speakingInst1: There are 8 speaking tasks with different prompts.
test.speakingInst2: In this mock test, type your spoken response instead of recording.
test.speakingInst3: Each task has a preparation period and a response period.
test.speakingInst4: Try to respond as naturally and fully as you would when speaking.
test.question: Q {current}/{total}

results.title: Your Results
results.subtitle: PugPIP Score Report
results.aiScoringDesc: AI is evaluating your writing and speaking responses. Scores will update automatically.
results.scoreRange: CELPIP scores range from M (minimal) to 12 (advanced)
results.scoreNote: Note: Writing and speaking scores are estimated based on response length only. In the actual CELPIP test, these sections are scored by trained raters who evaluate content, coherence, vocabulary, and grammar.

reviewPage.noDetailedAnswersDesc: This test was taken before answer tracking was enabled. Future tests will include full answer review.
